title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity · History Brief: Gita Loose Pack Delta Storage
summary: A long-lived repository grows unexpectedly, contains unreachable objects, and reports a missing tree after interrupted maintenance; the team needs causal storage evidence before cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita loose pack delta storage increment in object-database integrity and reachability report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Loose Pack Delta Storage
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G76 is a theory in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Loose Pack Delta Storage contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G76 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation. A defensible solution applies Gita Loose Pack Delta Storage, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to object-database integrity and reachability report.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 3
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A deltified object has an ID derived from its delta instructions. This fails the Gita Loose Pack Delta Storage contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Gita Loose Pack Delta Storage evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-loose-pack-delta-storage-1wj671" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Loose Pack Delta Storage into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-loose-pack-delta-storage-1wj671
# scenario-key: trace-1wj671 probe-176jw1 boundary-j6711w owner-1wj671x fault-1wj671z
# before-ref: main=a11ce1w candidate=b00b171
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11ce1w candidate=c0ffee1
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
